title: Award Win Reinforces Drekford’s Reputation in Nigeria’s Competitive Property Market
description: Award Win Reinforces Drekford’s Reputation in Nigeria’s Competitive Property Market  nigeriahousingmarket.com
content: Drekford Wins Real Estate Award as Firm Expands Footprint in Nigeria’s Property Market JUN 16 WRITTEN BY AYOMIDE FIYINFUNOLUWA Drekford Wins Prestigious Real Estate Award for Sector Contributions Drekford has reinforced its standing in Nigeria’s real estate sector after receiving a prestigious industry award that recognises its contributions to property development, innovation and market growth. The recognition reflects the company’s expanding presence within the country's competitive real estate landscape and highlights its role in delivering residential and investment-focused property solutions. / You Might Also Like / The 2026 Dangote Refinery Blueprint: Location, IPO, and Global Energy Impact The Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway: Transforming Nigeria’s Infrastructure Landscape Nigeria’s Housing Deficit: How Big Is the Problem in 2026? Mistakes First-Time Property Investors Make in Nigeria The award comes at a time when Nigeria’s property sector continues to evolve amid rising demand for housing, urban expansion and growing investor interest in real estate assets. Industry observers say such recognitions often serve as indicators of corporate performance, market credibility and long-term commitment to sector development. Discover more Geographic Reference Construction Consulting & Contracting Rental Recognition for Industry Excellence According to the organisers, the award acknowledges Drekford’s achievements in delivering quality real estate projects and maintaining high professional standards within the industry. The company has built a reputation around property development, investment opportunities and customer-focused housing solutions, positioning itself among emerging players in Nigeria’s growing real estate market. Industry awards are increasingly viewed as important benchmarks that recognise innovation, operational excellence and contributions to economic development within the property sector. Growing Presence in Nigeria’s Property Market The recognition highlights Drekford’s efforts to strengthen its footprint in a market characterised by increasing demand for residential housing and real estate investment products. Discover more Rental Construction & Maintenance Residential Rentals Nigeria’s housing sector continues to face a significant supply gap, creating opportunities for developers that can deliver quality projects while addressing affordability and infrastructure challenges. As urbanisation accelerates across major cities, developers are increasingly focusing on housing delivery, land development and investment-driven projects to meet changing market needs. Drekford’s growing visibility within the sector reflects broader trends of expansion and competition among private developers seeking to capture opportunities within the real estate market. Real Estate Remains Key Investment Asset The award also underscores the importance of real estate as a long-term investment vehicle in Nigeria. Despite economic challenges, property remain

This reinforces Drekford's standing in the market, particularly in delivering residential and investment-focused property solutions. The award highlights the growing demand for housing and urban expansion in Nigeria, which is expected to continue in 2026.

title: Drekford strengthens market position with real estate award
description: Drekford strengthens market position with real estate award  Business News Nigeria
content: Share Discover more Economic Research Reports Aviation Industry News Banking Sector Analysis Drekford Global Concept Limited, has emerged as the Residential Property Development Company of the Year at the Abuja Real Estate Fireside Chat 3.0 and ARE Awards. This was in recognition of its contributions to quality housing delivery and commitment to excellence in Nigeria’s real estate sector. The award was presented during the Abuja Real Estate Fireside Chat 3.0 and ARE Awards ceremony held at the Family Worship Centre in Wuye, Abuja, where key stakeholders across the property and construction industry gathered to celebrate outstanding achievements and discuss the future of real estate development in the country. The event, themed “Built to Last,” attracted leading developers, investors, real estate agents, construction professionals and other industry players who examined emerging trends, explored opportunities for collaboration and recognised innovation driving growth within the sector. Speaking shortly after receiving the award, Daniel Oparinde, Chief Executive Officer of Drekford Global Concept Limiited, described the recognition as a testament to the company’s unwavering commitment to delivering quality, sustainable and durable housing projects. According to Oparinde, the achievement reflects the collective efforts of the company’s workforce and the confidence reposed in the organisation by its clients and business partners. “This award reflects the dedication and hard work of our team and our unwavering commitment to delivering quality developments that stand the test of time. We will continue to raise industry standards and create lasting value for our clients and communities,” he said. The CEO also expressed gratitude to the organisers of the Abuja Real Estate Fireside Chat, led by Mark Onyekachi, for providing a platform that promotes professional excellence and encourages growth across the real estate industry. He further acknowledged the contributions of the company’s employees, clients, partners and supporters, noting that their commitment and trust played a significant role in securing the award. title: How to buy land in Nigeria without falling victim to fraud
description: How to buy land in Nigeria without falling victim to fraud  TheCable
content: Stay connected via Google News Many Nigerians living abroad consider buying land back home to be more than an investment. It is often the realisation of a long-held dream. The Nigerian diaspora continues to invest in land, whether to build a family home, establish a business, prepare for retirement or simply maintain a connection with their roots. Unfortunately, it is also one of the most vulnerable areas for fraud, where many innocent buyers lose a lot of money every year. The allure of owning property in Nigeria is understandable. In many parts of the country, particularly in rapidly growing urban and suburban areas, land values continue to climb. However, the very opportunities that attract genuine investors also appeal to fraudsters who take advantage of the distance, trust and limited access to information that often characterise diaspora transactions. A constant challenge for Nigerians abroad is the inability to physically inspect properties before making payments. Many, therefore, depend heavily on relatives, friends, estate agents or developers who say they have found suitable opportunities. These people may be acting in good faith, but their involvement does not relieve the obligation to properly verify. Indeed, many fraudulent transactions have happened precisely because buyers have blindly trusted third parties, without carrying out the necessary due diligence. One common example is when a potential buyer is shown photos and videos of a parcel of land through social media or messaging apps. Often, the seller creates a sense of urgency about the offer, claiming there are several interested buyers for the property and that payment must be made immediately to secure the purchase. In the race to buy land and the fear of missing out, some buyers transfer huge amounts of money without even verifying the ownership and authenticity of the documents shown to them. They later find that the seller had no legal right to sell the land, the property had been sold to someone else, or the land itself is subject to competing claims and disputes. Advertisement The fraud of land in Nigeria has many sides. Some people pretend to be owners when they are actually caretakers, family members, tenants or agents without the authority to transfer title. At other times, forged title documents such as Certificates of Occupancy, survey plans and deeds of assignment are produced to look real. There have also been many cases where one piece of land has been sold to more than one purchaser, each receiving a deed purporting to establish ownership. These risks underline the importance of thorough investigations before spending money on any property transaction. Prospective buyers should always try to confirm ownership and the legal status of the land, no matter how convincing a seller is or how tempting the offer. A title search will tell you if the seller owns the property, if there are any encumbrances on the property, if the land is subject to acquisition by the gove
summary: The article reports on the common practice of land fraud in Nigeria, particularly affecting diaspora buyers who invest in land without proper verification. This issue is relevant to the Lagos market, where land values are increasing in rapidly growing urban and suburban areas, with many buyers losing money due to fraudulent transactions. According to the article, many buyers transfer huge amounts of money without verifying the ownership and authenticity of documents, with some losing up to an unspecified amount.
trending_topics: land fraud, diaspora investment, Lagos real estate

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The article highlights the need for Mixta Africa to educate customers on the risks of land fraud and the importance of due diligence in property transactions.

title: ‘Economic stability crucial for luxury real estate growth’
description: ‘Economic stability crucial for luxury real estate growth’  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: ‘Economic stability crucial for luxury real estate growth’ By : Victor Gbonegun Date: 15 June 2026 4:38am WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover 7-Fifteen Capital 7-Fifteen Capital A Private developer, 7-Fifteen Capital Limited, has said sustained economic stability and market predictability are critical to the growth of Nigeria’s luxury real estate sector. Speaking at the launch of the company’s Lagos office, the Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder, Tope Adekoya, said foreign exchange volatility and rising inflation had significantly increased construction costs in recent years, posing major challenges for developers. According to him, projects initially valued at $10 million now cost nearly double due to the heavy reliance on imported construction materials. “The luxury real estate market has faced difficult conditions over the past six years because of exchange rate fluctuations and inflation. Developers must adopt creative financing models and focus on building functional, right-sized homes that meet market needs,” he said. Adekoya noted that the outlook for the luxury housing market would largely depend on economic policies and exchange rate stability, which provide developers with greater certainty over project costs. He said the company’s expansion into Lagos follows successful developments in Abuja, including Myari Lakeside, The Residence and The Cove Lakeside, adding that the firm remains committed to delivering affordable luxury housing. Related News Hamzat-Sonayon-James ticket and future of Lagos governance Alimosho APC seeks Tinubu’s intervention over alleged plans to overturn primary result Sanwo-Olu scouts climate investment in London Also speaking, Deputy Chief Executive Officer and Co-Founder, Adesuwa Belo-Osagie, said the company intends to contribute to community development through its foundation by supporting education and creating opportunities for young people. Group Chief Executive Officer, Tolu Anyakoya, said the firm’s projects focus on delivering quality living experiences through secure environments and reliable infrastructure, including electricity, water supply and security services. He disclosed that the company currently has about 10 estates under development in Abuja and two projects in Lagos, located in Ikoyi and Banana Island. Lagos State Governor, Babajide Sanwo-Olu, stressed the importance of public-private partnerships in building world-class cities, noting that no government can achieve sustainable urban development alone. He said the private sector contributes capital, technology and speed, while the government provides the planning framework and enabling environment required for growth. Sanwo-Olu urged developers and investors to focus on projects that create lasting value for future generations, describing Lagos as a city of boundless opportunities driven by collaboration and innovation. Tags: 7-Fifteen Capital Limited Babajide Sanwo-Olu
summary: The article reports that 7-Fifteen Capital Limited, a private developer, emphasizes the need for economic stability and market predictability for the growth of Nigeria's luxury real estate sector. The company's CEO notes that foreign exchange volatility and rising inflation have increased construction costs, making projects valued at $10 million now cost nearly double. This implies that Lagos' luxury real estate market faces challenges due to economic instability, affecting developers' ability to deliver affordable luxury housing.
trending_topics: economic stability, luxury real estate, construction costs, foreign exchange volatility, inflation

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The article's focus on luxury real estate may not directly impact Mixta's projects, but it highlights the broader market challenges that Mixta may face in delivering its own luxury projects, such as Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The article's emphasis on economic instability and rising construction costs may pose a risk to Mixta's execution of its luxury projects, particularly if the company relies heavily on imported materials.
